items.title: AWS CloudFormation Introduction
items.summary: I have started writing about AWS CloudFormation which is one of the AWS IaC (Infrastructure as Code) tools made available for free by AWS.

items.title: Java Generics - Updated
items.summary: I have started updating my Java Generics tutorial

items.title: AWS Core Technologies
items.summary: With hundreds of AWS services and technologies available, it can be hard to figure out which technologies to learn first, and which can wait. Therefore I have published a list of what I consider core AWS technologies which I believe it is good to know early in your AWS journey. These technologies are often used, so learning them will be beneficial.

items.title: AWS Introduction
items.summary: If you are just beginning your AWS journey - figuring out which AWS services to learn first, and how to interact best with AWS can be a daunting task. Therefore I have published a short introduction to AWS which lists the bare essentials to guide you on this AWS journey.

items.title: Core Software Performance Optimization Principles
items.summary: This tutorial lists the core 9 + 1 software performance optimization principles I use when working with software performance optimization.

items.title: Developer Learning Strategies
items.summary: As developer we have to learn a lot continuously. Learning can sometimes be frustrating. I have published an article explaining my developer learning strategies - for dealing with the times when learning is hard.

items.title: P2P Network Introduction Updated
items.summary: I have updated my P2P network introduction with information about structured and unstructured P2P network topologies (such as Gnutella).

items.title: HTML5 Tutorial - Introduction Fixed
items.summary: I have fixed a small error in the introduction to the HTML5 Canvas - the powerful HTML5 element that enables you to draw free form graphics directly in the browser.

items.title: Essential Dev Skills
items.summary: I have made a list of skills / topics I believe most developers will benefit from having / knowing about to some extent at least.

items.title: What is new in Java 18?
items.summary: I have updated the Java version / feature list to list what is new in Java 18.

items.title: What is new in Java 17?
items.summary: I have updated the Java version / feature list to list what is new in Java 17.

items.title: What is new in Java 16?
items.summary: I have updated the Java version / feature list to list what is new in Java 16.

items.title: SVG Tutorials Moved
items.summary: I have moved my SVG tutorials from tutorials.jenkov.com to jenkov.com

items.title: CSS Tutorials Moved
items.summary: I have moved my CSS tutorials from tutorials.jenkov.com to jenkov.com

items.title: Maven Tutorials Moved
items.summary: I have moved my Maven tutorials from tutorials.jenkov.com to jenkov.com

items.title: Peer-to-peer Tutorials Moved
items.summary: I have moved my Peer-to-Peer (P2P) tutorials from tutorials.jenkov.com to jenkov.com

items.title: GraalVM JavaScript Interpreter
items.summary: GraalVM comes with a JavaScript interpreter which you can use to run JavaScript both from the command line and from inside your Java code. GraalVM also has Node.js + NPM compatible commands you can install - if you want or need that. In this tutorial I explain how to use the GraalVM JavaScript interpreter, and how to install the Node.JS + NPM commands.

items.title: Polymorph Introduction
items.summary: Polymorph is my own project within the smart, decentralized media space. The project is now concrete enough that I was able to write an introduction to what Polymorph is, and explain the bigger picture of what it will do and how it will work.

items.title: Decentralized Systems - Updated
items.summary: I have updated the tutorial on decentralized systems to also list some of the most common benefits and challenges of decentralized systems.

items.title: Decentralized Systems
items.summary: I have published a short introduction to decentralized systems such as decentralized server systems like DHT (Distributed Hashtables), P2P networks, Blockchain systems etc.

items.title: GraalVM Python Interpreter
items.summary: GraalVM has a Python interpreter you can install which enables you to run Python programs on GraalVM or execute Python from within your Java code. This short tutorial explains how to install the GraalVM Python interpreter - and how to run a Python program from the command line.

items.title: GraalVM Polyglot API
items.summary: I have added 2 small examples of how to execute JavaScript from inside a Java app via the GraalVM Polyglot API.

items.title: GraalVM Tutorial
items.summary: I have started writing a tutorial about GraalVM - the VM that can run Java, JavaScript, Python, Ruby, LLVM and Web Assembly in the same application.

items.title: Blockchain Projects and Resources
items.summary: I have started making a list of the most interesting and popular blockchain projects out there, to help beginners get started on their crypto journeys.

items.title: Blockchain Introduction
items.summary: Blockchain technology is seeing an increase in use cases it can address, in addition to just crypto currency. Therefore I have started studying blockchain technology, and also started writing about it. So far I have published a short introduction to blockchain. More will follow in the future.

items.title: Thread Congestion
items.summary: If multiple threads try to access the same, blocking data structure at the same time, often, this can lead to thread congestion, which may lead to performance degradation.

items.title: Single-threaded and Same-threaded Designs
items.summary: Single-threaded and same-threaded designs can be very powerful if you understand how to design them properly. I have updated this tutorial with a video edition.

items.title: Polymorph P2P Network Algorithm
items.summary: The Polymorph P2P network algorithm is based on my own, new P2P polyring topology designed especially with media routing in mind. I believe it is better suited at media routing than Chord, Kademlia, Pastry and Taspestry.

items.title: Chord P2P Network Algorithm
items.summary: The Chord P2P network algorithm was one of the early P2P algorithms to appear. This Chord tutorial explains the core of the Chord P2P algorithm.

items.title: Java Annotations
items.summary: I have updated my Java Annotations tutorial to also explain the built-in annotation @Contended.

items.title: False Sharing in Java
items.summary: False sharing is a situation that can cause performance degradations when two or more threads write to different variables located within the same CPU cache line.

items.title: CPU Cache Coherence in Java Concurrency
items.summary: In this short tutorial I correct my statements about how Java synchronized and volatile variables effect how thread visible variables are flushed to main RAM.

items.title: Compare and Swap in Java
items.summary: Compare and swap functionality is an alternative to synchronized blocks in Java when designing high throughput, non-blocking concurrent algorithms in Java.

items.title: Peer Findability and Message Routability
items.summary: The peer findability and message routability challenges of P2P networks are typically addressed in similar ways. This tutorial covers common solutions to peer findability and message routability in peer-to-peer networks.

items.title: Peer Addressability
items.summary: Peers in a peer-to-peer network need to be able to address each other uniquely. That it was peer addressability refers to. This tutorial explains how common P2P algorithms approaches peer addressability.

items.title: Peer Connectivity
items.summary: Peer connectivity tutorial out! Explains a common solution to overcoming peers behind NAT and firewall - so all peers in a P2P network can communicate with each other.

items.title: Peer-to-peer Networks - Update Started
items.summary: A lot has happened in the world of Peer-to-Peer (P2P) networks since I wrote the first version of this tutorial - so I have started updating it. First step was updating the introduction.

items.title: Single-threaded Concurrency
items.summary: Single-threaded concurrency designs are an alternative to multithreaded concurrency designs which achieves many of the same benefits (task switching) without many of the multithreading problems (thread update visibility, race conditions etc.etc.).

items.title: Java BlockingQueue
items.summary: The Java BlockingQueue interface represents a queue which can block threads from inserting into a full queue, or block threads from dequeuing from an empty queue.

items.title: Deadlock Prevention in Java
items.summary: In this tutorial I explain 3 common methods for preventing deadlock occurring. I have also made a video version of this tutorial.

items.title: Deadlock in Java
items.summary: Deadlock is when two or more threads end up blocked indefinitely waiting for each other to release resources they need to lock to do their job. I have updated the Deadlock tutorial.

items.title: Java ExecutorService
items.summary: The Java ExecutorService is a thread pool built into Java. I have update the Java ExecutorService tutorial, including added a new video version of this tutorial.

items.title: JavaFX TableView - Editable Cells
items.summary: I have updated my JavaFX TableView tutorial to explain how to make table cells editable.

items.title: Java Lock
items.summary: The Java Lock interface represents an lock which can make sure only one thread can lock it at a time - similarly to how a Java synchronized blocks works - only more flexible.

items.title: Thread Pools in Java
items.summary: Thread pools are an alternative to creative one thread per task to execute. In this thread pool tutorial I explain how thread pools work, as well as how to implement your own in Java. I have also added a video version of this tutorial.

items.title: JavaFX Transformations
items.summary: You can apply transformations such as rotation, translation and scaling to all JavaFX controls. This JavaFX transformations tutorial explains how JavaFX transformations work.

items.title: JavaFX Button
items.summary: The JavaFX Button class provides a button that users of your JavaFX app can click on. I have updated my JavaFX Button tutorial to explain how to use a Button from within JavaFX FXML.

items.title: Concurrency vs Parallelism
items.summary: Concurrency and parallelism are two similar, yet different concepts. I have rewritten this tutorial to better explain the difference between concurrency and parallelism - plus I have added a video version of this tutorial.

items.title: JavaFX Fonts
items.summary: The JavaFX Font class enables you to load and use different fonts in your JavaFX applications.

items.title: Java Exception Handling
items.summary: I have updated my introduction to Java Exception Handling - the way you handle errors and exceptions in Java.

items.title: Race Conditions in Java
items.summary: I have updated the Race Conditions in Java tutorial, with a video version among other changes.

items.title: JavaFX Pane
items.summary: The JavaFX Pane class is a simple layout container which lays out its child components at exactly their layoutX and layoutY property values.

items.title: JavaFX Region
items.summary: The JavaFX Region class is a base class for several JavaFX layout container classes like Pane, HBox and VBox. The JavaFX Region properties are thus shared by these subclasses.

items.title: JavaFX TableView
items.summary: The JavaFX TableView is able to display tabular data in a grid-like table. I have updated this JavaFX TableView tutorial to also explain how to use Java Map instances as data items in a TableView.

items.title: Veteran, Senior and Junior Developers
items.summary: In this new video I explain why I believe there is a developer experience level beyond senior developer, which I refer to as veteran developer.

items.title: Maven Memory Limits
items.summary: You can set the memory limits of Maven when building your Maven projects. This Maven memory limits tutorial explains how.

items.title: Maven Surefire
items.summary: The Maven Surefire plugin executes your Maven project's unit tests. This Maven Surefire tutorial has been updated to explain how to execute unit tests with Java preview features enabled.

items.title: Java Path in Java NIO
items.summary: The Java NIO Path class represents a file system path. The Path class can help you do various path related calculations, such as expressing one Path as a relative path from another Path.

items.title: Java Comparator
items.summary: The Java Comparator interface represents a component that is able to compare two objects, so the objects can be sorted using Java's built-in sorting functionality.

items.title: Java Comparable
items.summary: The Java Comparable interface is intended to make your classes comparable, so you can sort them using Java's built-in sorting functionality.
